channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Colombian president aims to reduce drug violence and weaken rebels by paying farmers to switch crops
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro announced a plan Monday to reduce coca planting in a northeastern region rattled by rebel attacks by paying farmers while they switch to legal crops.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KRON4

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Storms, possible twisters to threaten the South just as New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms with a threat of tornadoes are expected to punch through Louisiana and other parts of the South on Tuesday just as costumed revelers celebrate Mardi Gras with huge parades and partying in the streets of New Orleans and other cities in the region.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAA opens probe on why flights approaching Ronald Reagan airport received false alerts of nearby aircraft
item.description: The FAA has launched a probe into why flights approaching the Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport received erroneous alerts of other aircraft in the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New charge filed against son of Buc-ee's co-founder
item.description: Mitchell Wasek, 29, is being charged with a third-degree felony offense of possession or promotion of child pornography, with a $50,000 bond.
item.dc:creator: Dalton Huey, NewsNation

item.title: California Highway Patrol blames drug use, speeding for fiery Cybertruck crash that killed 3
item.description: PIEDMONT, Calif. (AP) — Drug use and excessive speed were to blame for last year's fiery crash of a Tesla Cybertruck that killed three college students near San Francisco, the California Highway Patrol said in a preliminary report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kroger Chairman and CEO resigns following investigation into personal conduct
item.description: Kroger Chairman and CEO Rodney McMullen has resigned after an internal investigation into his personal conduct.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 175 wildfires in North and South Carolina force some evacuations
item.description: Wildfires in North and South Carolina have scorched thousands of acres as crews battling more than 175 blazes face dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at the Oscars
item.description: "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Daryl Hannah voices support for Ukraine on Oscars stage
item.description: The 64-year-old performer's brief remark came days after an explosive confrontation between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Rubio says Trump would be 'on his way to the Nobel Peace Prize' if he were a Democrat
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio fiercely defended President Trump’s approach to negotiating a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia on Sunday, saying the media public would be praising his efforts if he were a Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Oscars 2025: How to watch, and when the show begins
item.description: The Oscars themselves will start at 7 p.m. EST but red carpet coverage has already kicked off on ABC and Hulu, E!, and The Associated Press. The official Oscars preshow on ABC and Hulu will begin at 6:30 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Tributes pour in for R&B singer Angie Stone after her death at 63 in a highway crash
item.description: Fans are remembering the voice and songs of trailblazer Angie Stone after her death at 63 in a weekend crash on an Alabama highway as she traveled from a performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee could add 'covenant marriage' with proposed bill
item.description: One Tennessee lawmaker would like to add a new kind of marriage to the books through a bill filed in the General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, ABC 4

item.title: About 8 in 10 say they've recently experienced extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Overall, nearly 70 percent of adults believe in climate change, with 93 percent of Democrats and 51 percent of Republicans saying so, poll results show.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KRON4

item.title: Lee Kunzman, 'persistent' Indy 500 veteran, dies at 80
item.description: Kunzman's best finish in his INDYCAR SERIES career was in 1979, when he placed second at Atlanta Motor Speedway. It was just a narrow loss to winner Johnny Rutherford, IMS officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Renowned geneticist Francis Collins retires from NIH, urging 'respect' for embattled workers
item.description: Dr. Francis Collins, a renowned geneticist and former longtime director of the National Institutes of Health, announced Saturday that he has retired, leaving an agency being upended by budget cuts and layoffs.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black graves are being moved to make way for an industrial park, drawing a mix of emotions
item.description: The graves of hundreds of African American tenant farmers and their families are being moved in Virginia to make way for an industrial park.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alabama governor commutes death row inmate Rocky Myers' sentence to life in prison
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey commuted the death sentence of Robin “Rocky” Myers to life in prison Friday, saying there were enough questions about his guilt that she could not move forward with his execution.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah senators react to contentious Oval Office meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Utah's senators are responding to a contentious interaction between Trump and Vance and Zelenskyy Friday with contrasting public statements.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Trump-Zelensky clash highlights Congress at a crossroads over foreign policy
item.description: The explosive clash between President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the White House on Friday has sparked a firestorm on Capitol Hill, pitting “America First” Republicans against Ukraine backers from both parties in the ferocious battle over the future of U.S. foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Republicans target SNAP in federal spending cuts; food banks brace for impact
item.description: As House Republicans push to cut billions from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly known as food stamps, food banks are bracing for potential impacts.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Volunteers use bullhorns and sirens to warn immigrants when ICE is in their area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just before dawn, 10 people met at a parking lot shared by a laundromat and coffee shop in South Central Los Angeles on what has become a daily mission: Look for immigration officers and warn people of their presence to try to prevent arrests.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As Trump's deadline to eliminate DEI nears, few schools openly rush to make changes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools and colleges across the U.S. face a Friday deadline to end diversity programs or risk having their federal money pulled by the Trump administration, yet few are openly rushing to make changes.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, JOCELYN GECKER and CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, ABC 4
